content:
[Goal]

- The first paragraph of the problem statement directly contradicts the rest of the problem statement on whether or not the mapping from switches to bulbs is known to the player. If the context given in the first paragraph is not used in the rest of the clash, it is better to omit the first paragraph for brevity.

- (Suggestion for clarity) Please format "0" and "1" as constants.

[Input]

- For clarity, please add "1, 2, and 3 respectively" to the end of the first two lines (e.g., "Three space-separated integers [[b1]], [[b2]], and [[b3]] representing the initial states of bulbs 1, 2, and 3 respectively ({{0}} = OFF, {{1}} = ON).").

- Please rephrase line 3 as "Three space-separated integers [[m1]], [[m2]], and [[m3]] describing the mapping: switch 1 controls bulb [[m1]], switch 2 controls bulb [[m2]], and switch 3 controls bulb [[m3]]."

- (Suggestion for clarity) Please format "0" and "1" as constants.

[Output]

- Please add that the output of the states of the three bulbs should be space-separated integers (each {{0}} or {{1}}).

[Constraints]

- Consider removing the first three constraints; they are all stated in the Input section and/or implied by the Input description.

[Other Suggestions]

- Consider enabling Shortest mode for this clash :)

Thank you!
